title: Spec / design-direction
description: Aesthetic constraints and references that feed the design-director agent in Phase 2a. The agent uses this to generate 3 directions; the operator picks one.

aesthetic_keywords.description: Adjectives the client used or that capture the desired feel (e.g. "industrial", "precise", "warm", "editorial"). Min 3 to force articulation, max 12 to prevent dilution.

mood.description: One-sentence emotional summary of the desired site (e.g. "sebevědomá technická firma, která ví, co dělá").

description.description: Short note on what this reference is.

like_what.description: What specifically the client likes about it (typography, layout, colour, motion, …).

weight.description: How influential this reference should be.

dislikes.description: Things the client explicitly does not want ("corporate stock photos", "playful illustrations", "sliders").

palette_type.description: Constraint on palette shape; "open" means no constraint.

must_include.description: Hex colours that must appear (typically existing brand colours).
Specific font families that must be used (e.g. existing brand font).

imagery_style.description: Dominant imagery family. "minimal-no-imagery" is an explicit choice, not absence of decision.

motion_preference.description: How much motion the operator wants. The animation-polish-agent in Phase 7 honours this.
